topic.name: 30 Rock Episodes

topic.name: Songs and Their Themes
topic.description: This cluster discusses various songs, their musical composition, lyrical themes, and cultural significance.

topic.name: 19th Century Ironclad Warships
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, construction, and service history of various ironclad warships from the 19th century.

Document 1: Warrior-class ironclads were a class of two warships built for the Royal Navy between 1859 and 1862
The ships were designed as armoured frigates in response to an invasion scare
Document 2: HMS Zealous was one of the three ships forming the second group of wooden steam battleships selected in 1860 for conversion to ironclads
This was done in response to the perceived threat to Britain offered by the large French ironclad building programme
Document 3: ARA Almirante Brown was a central battery ironclad of the Argentine Navy built in the 1880s

topic.name: Infrastructure and Social Issues
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of infrastructure development and the social implications surrounding it, including transportation routes and activism against economic policies.

Document 1: State Route 168 is a legislated, but not constructed, state highway located in Washington, United States.
The highway would allow commercial vehicles to bypass Mount Rainier National Park.
Document 2: The October Coalition demanded an end to structural adjustment policies believed to prioritize profit over the lives of individuals.
Demonstrators expressed a critical view of the economic and immigration policies of the United States.
Document 3: The DeKalb County Courthouse is the county's primary judicial center and is a contributing property to the Sycamore Historic District.
The current building was constructed in 1905 amid controversy over where the courthouse and thus, ultimately, the county seat would be located.

topic.name: Hurricane Impacts and Responses
topic.description: The documents discuss various hurricanes and their effects, including meteorological details and community responses to disaster situations.

Document 1: Hurricane Helene was the ninth tropical storm, fourth hurricane, and strongest hurricane of the 2006 Atlantic hurricane season.
It never affected land until the very end of its lifespan as a weak extratropical system, which had minor impact in the northern British Isles.
Document 2: Hurricane Lili was one of only five Atlantic tropical cyclones on record to reach hurricane status in the month of December.
The storm produced light rainfall but no damage.
Document 3: The New Waveland Café and New Waveland Clinic together formed a disaster response center... that operated free of charge from September 5 to December 1, 2005 in immediate Post-Katrina Mississippi Gulf Coast.
They were founded in response to Hurricane Katrina and provided free food and free medical care to hurricane victims for three months.

topic.name: Major League Baseball Players

Bowker was drafted by the San Francisco Giants and became the first San Francisco-era Giant to hit a home run in his first two major league games.

topic.name: Greco-Persian Wars
topic.description: The documents discuss key battles and conflicts between Greek city-states and the Persian Empire during the Greco-Persian Wars, specifically focusing on the Delian League's military engagements.

Document 1: The Battle of the Eurymedon was a double battle between the Delian League of Athens and her Allies, and the Persian Empire of Xerxes I.
It forms part of the Wars of the Delian League, itself part of the larger Greco-Persian Wars.
Document 2: The Battle of Lade was a naval battle which occurred during the Ionian Revolt, fought between an alliance of the Ionian cities and the Persian Empire of Darius the Great.
The Ionian Revolt was triggered by the dissatisfaction of the Greek cities of Asia Minor with the tyrants appointed by Persia to rule them.
Document 3: The Wars of the Delian League were a series of campaigns fought between the Delian League of Athens and her allies, and the Achaemenid Empire of Persia.
These conflicts represent a continuation of the Greco-Persian Wars, after the Ionian Revolt and the first and second Persian invasions of Greece.
